reasons: introduces proof-pr as a reusable CI evidence consumer

change.summary: Adds an advisory proof-pr dogfood workflow and committed receipt for GitHub Repo Auditor.
change.scope_notes: No application code, package metadata, generated portfolio truth, or release workflow behavior changes.

evidence.summary: Committed receipt validates against proof-pr.v1 schema.
Receipt renders into the standard Markdown PR block.
Existing public fixture proof package remains valid.
No leaks found in the dogfood worktree.
No private repo names, local paths, personal email, or token prefixes found in the new public dogfood files.
No Python source, package metadata, or generated truth surface changed.
evidence.reason: The PR adds only an advisory workflow caller and proof receipt; normal CI still runs on pull_request.

evidence.summary: No UI, workbook, dashboard, or visual artifact changed.

permission_diff.summary: New workflow grants explicit read-only permissions: contents: read and actions: read.

redaction.summary: No screenshots or generated portfolio artifacts are included in this PR.

rollback.path: Revert this PR or remove .github/workflows/proof-pr.yml and proof-pr.json.
rollback.notes: The workflow is manual-only and advisory, so rollback does not require data migration or external cleanup.

limitations: The committed receipt uses pending-pr-head because a receipt committed in the same change cannot know its final commit SHA before the commit exists.
PR number and PR URL should be reflected in the pull request proof block after PR creation.
The workflow is manual-only for dogfood; pull_request enforcement is intentionally deferred.
